response.justification: The candidate is a strong senior full-stack engineer with deep web, Node.js/TypeScript, and DLang experience, but the role centers on hands-on C++ development for industrial automation. His only professional C++ work was a ~7-month stint at National Instruments in 2014-2015, and the resume shows no MQTT, message-broker, IEC 62443/SDL, or industrial-protocol experience. While total experience exceeds the 8-12 year range and he has relevant systems concepts (low-latency, event-driven, concurrency via DLang fibers), the core C++/industrial-cybersecurity profile is largely absent.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software engineering experience across web, embedded, and high-throughput backend systems
Low-latency, high-performance systems work (async I/O, caching, fibers) at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic
Strong engineering craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, mentoring, workshops, and open-source leadership
concerns: Minimal professional C++/STL experience — one short role in 2014-2015 plus a thesis project
No industrial automation, IEC 62443, SDL, MQTT, or OT-protocol background (Modbus, OPC UA, etc.)
Recent career trajectory is full-stack web/AI tooling, diverging from the C++ platform-engineering focus of this role

key_factors.factor: Strong general systems and concurrency engineering background
